title: Flamelet_based_modelinf_of_NO_fomration_in_turbuelnt_hydrogen_diffusion_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical information extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   EINOx = EINO(MNO2 /MNO) + EINO2
*   EINO = (2 * PI * D^3 * Integral[0 to x] d(r/D) * Integral[r/D to x] (r/D) * wNO d(r/D)) / (1/4 * PI * rho_f * U_j^2 * D^2 * Y_f) = (8*D) / (rho_f * U_j * Y_f)
*   I ~ (1/D^3) * Integral Integral Integral( wNO dV)
*   EINOx,eq ~ D/Uj
*   I ~ wNO, max (zeta = zeta_st)
*   zeta ~ Uj/D
*   EINOx ~ (D/Uj)^(1/2)
*   I ~ (Uj/D)^(1/2)
*   wNO ~ C0CN2 exp(-Ta/T)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters**

*   rho_f: density of the nozzle gas (fuel)
*   Y_f:  fuel mass fraction in the fuel stream
*   U_j: appropriate powers, independent of it
*   D: appropriate powers, independent of it
*   zeta: mean scalar dissipation rate or large eddy strain rate
*   Ta: activation temperature (approximately 38,000 K)
*   C0: oxygen radical concentration
*   CN2: nitrogen molecule concentration
*   zeta_max : wNO, max exhibits a maximum at this

title: Flamelet_based_modelinf_of_NO_fomration_in_turbuelnt_hydrogen_diffusion_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   strain rate scales as x^-2
*   scalar dissipation rate scales as x^-4

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Axes:**
    *   X-axis: Strain rate (1/s), logarithmic scale from 1 to 10000
    *   Y-axis left: Tmax(K), linear scale from 0 to 3000
    *   Y-axis right: w\_NO (kg/m3/s) and X\_O, linear scale from 0.00 to 0.10
*   **Curves:**
    *   T (temperature): Starts at about 2400K, rises to ~2700K between 10-100 strain rate, gradually decreases to ~2300K at 10000 strain rate.
    *   W\_NO (NO production rate): Rises sharply to max value of ~0.07 kg/m3/s between strain rate 10-100 1/s, then falls to almost 0 at 10000.
    *   10*X\_O (O radical mole fraction): Small curve at ~o from strain rate 1 to strain rate 100, begins a steep curve upwards between 100-1000, curve flattens off after ~1000
*   **Key Values:**
    *   Maximum NO production rate (at peak of W\_NO curve).
    *   Maximum Temperature (at peak of T curve)

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   s\_max = 25 s^-1 (strain rate at maximum NO production for pure hydrogen flames)
*   X\_max ≈ 0.1 s^-1 (scalar dissipation rate at stoichiometric mixture)
*   Uj/D (ratio, used in context of turbulence and experimental trends)
*   Damköhler number (simulations conducted over seven decades of this number)

title: Flamelet_based_modelinf_of_NO_fomration_in_turbuelnt_hydrogen_diffusion_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text, organized by category:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ∂(¯pU)/∂x + (1/r) ∂(r ¯pV)/дr = 0                     (5)
*   ∂(¯pUU)/∂x + (1/r) ∂(r ¯pUV)/∂r = - ∂¯p/∂x - (1/r) ∂(r ¯pu"v")/∂r + Mol.Dif. (6)
*    ∂(¯pUF)/∂x + (1/r) ∂(r ¯pVF)/∂r = - (1/r) ∂(r ¯pu"f")/∂r + Mol.Dif. (7)
*   ∂/∂xₖ (¯pUk u"ᵢu"ⱼ ) =  Pᵢⱼ + Dᵢⱼ + p' (∂u"ᵢ/∂xⱼ + ∂u"ⱼ/∂xᵢ) - (2/3) δᵢⱼ ρε (8)
*    Pᵢⱼ = - ¯p (u"ᵢu"ₖ ∂Uⱼ/∂xₖ + u"ⱼu"ₖ ∂Uᵢ/∂xₖ ) (9)
*   Dᵢⱼ = Cₛ ∂/∂xₖ [k/ε (¯p - u"ₖu"ˡ + ¯p δₖ₁ ν) ∂u"ᵢu"ⱼ/∂x₁] (10)
*    k = (1/2)  u"ᵢu"ᵢ (11)
*   p' (∂u"ᵢ/∂xⱼ + ∂u"ⱼ/∂xᵢ) = Φᵢⱼ,₁ + Φᵢⱼ,₂ (12)
*   bᵢⱼ =  u"ᵢu"ⱼ / (2k) - (1/3) δᵢⱼ (13)
*   Φᵢⱼ,₁ = -C₁ ¯p ε 2bᵢⱼ (14)
*   Φᵢⱼ,₂ = -α (Pᵢⱼ - (2/3) δᵢⱼ P) (15)
*   P = (1/2) Pₖₖ (16)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Cs = Constant used in model A for diffusion term
*   C₁ = Constant used in the return term
*   α = Constant used in rapid term model
*   k = Turbulent kinetic energy per unit mass
*   ν = Molecular kinematic viscosity
*   ε = Dissipation rate
*   δᵢⱼ = Kronecker delta

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   d/dxk (p Uk u"i f") = Pi + Di + d/dxi (p' ui)
*   Pi = -p (u"i uk" (dUi/dxk) + u"i uk" (dF/dxk))
*   Di = Csf d/dxk (k/e p - u"i uk") d/dxi(u"i f")
*   d/dxi (p'ui) = phi_i,1 + phi_i,2
*   phi_i,1 = -C1f p  epsilon/k  u"i f"- C'1f p epsilon/k bik u"k f"
*   phi_i,2 = 0.8 p uk" f" (dUi/dxk) - 0.2 p uk" f" (dUk/dxk)
*   d/dx (p U phi) + 1/r d/dr (r p V phi) = 1/r d/dr (r p Gamma d phi/dr) + S_phi
*   epsilon_f = 2f n^2 epsilon/k

**2. Graphs/Charts:**  NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

|---|---|
| u” u" | -2(1 - α)ρu" v" ∂U/∂r - ⅔ ρε - C₁ ρ (u" u" ε/k - ⅔ k ) + ⅔ αP |
| v" v" |  ⅔ ρε - C₁ ρ (v" v" ε/k - ⅔ k ) + ⅔ αP - 2C₂ k ⅓ ρw" w" (v" v" - w" w") |
| w" w" | ⅔ ρε - C₁ ρ (w" w" ε/k - ⅔ k ) + ⅔ αP + 2C₂ k ⅓ ρw" w" (v" v" - w" w") |
| u” v" | -(1 - α)ρv" u" ∂U/∂r - C₁ ρv" u" ε/k -  Cs k/r² ρw" w" u" v" |
| ε | (Cε,1 P - Cε,2 ρε)/k |
| u” f" |  - ρu" v" ∂F/∂r - C₁f ρu" f" ε/k -(C₁f ρ ε/k ) (buu u"f" + buv v"f") + 0.8 ρu" u" ∂U/∂r |
| v” f" | -(ρv" u" ∂F/∂r + k/r ρw" w" ) - C₁f ρv" f" ε/k - Cₛ f ρ ε/kr²  u" v" f" |

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   `phi = integral from 0 to 1 of df integral from 0 to infinity of d zeta P(f, zeta) phi(f, zeta); (p_bar)^(-1) = integral from 0 to 1 of df integral from 0 to infinity of d zeta P(f, zeta) / rho(f, zeta)`
*   `C_1 = 1 + 0.5 exp(-7.77/Re_t^(1/2)) [72/Re_t^(1/2) + 80.1 ln(1 + 62.4(-II + 2.3III))] x (1/9 + 9II + 27III)`
*   `II = (1/2) (b_ij b_ji - Tr(b_ij^2)) = -(1/2) Tr(b_ij^2) = -(1/2) b_ij b_ji `
*   `III = (1/3!) (b_ij b_jk b_ki - 3b_ij Tr(b_ij^2) + 2Tr(b_ij^3)) = (2/3!) Tr(b_ij^3) = -(1/3!) b_ij b_jk b_ki`
*   `phi_ij, 2 = (2U_p / dx_q) (X_pj^qi + X_pj^qi)`
*   `X_pj, iso^qi = (2k/30)(4 * delta_pj * delta_iq - delta_ip * delta_jq - delta_pq * delta_jp )`
*   `X_pj, anis^qi = C_r alpha (delta_pq b_pj + (4/11) delta_pj b_iq - (3/11)Y_pj^qi) + (2k/11)(5 delta_pj b_iq - Y_pj^qi)`
*   Re_t = 4 * k^2 /(9 * epsilon * nu)
    `b_ij^2 = b_ik bkj`
    `b_ij^3 = b_ik b_kl b_lj`

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

| C1   | alpha | Ce | Ce,1 | Ce,2 | Ce,s | C1f | Ctf| Csf |
|------|-------|----|------|------|------|-----|----|-----|
| 2.3  | 0.6   | 0.22 | 1.45 | 1.90 | 0.18 | 5.7 |-6.1| 0.22|

Other values are:
Re = 4k²/9ev
